reason: Response A has the restrained, dry, observational voice with subtle deadpan absurdism and quietly landed emotional beats (the smaller second mat "hurt") that is characteristic of genuine Haiku creative writing. Response B leans on surface-level comic-rant markers (ALL-CAPS emphasis, heavy exclamation points, gross-out shock items, explicit "I have FEELINGS"), which reads more like an imitation exaggerating the obvious tropes of the form. The controlled economy and pathos of A feel more authentic than B's louder, more generic sitcom-rant register.
